summary: Operator-facing workflow for preparing an EU AI Act conformity assessment dossier (Article 43) using GovAI exports as supporting technical evidence. GovAI does not perform the legal assessment; this workflow structures the steps a provider follows internally before involving a notified body where one is required.

out_of_scope_notes: Prohibited AI practices under Article 5 are out of scope; this workflow assumes the system is not prohibited.
General-purpose AI obligations follow separate provider obligations and are not modeled here.

workflow_phases.title: System scoping and classification
objectives: Confirm intended purpose and reasonably foreseeable use.
Classify the system against Annex III categories and Article 6 conditions.
Decide whether internal production control or third-party assessment applies.

workflow_phases.title: Technical documentation (Annex IV) preparation
objectives: Assemble the elements required by Annex IV.
Reference GovAI evidence exports where they add traceability.

workflow_phases.title: Internal production control review
objectives: Verify quality management system coverage of the system.
Confirm risk management lifecycle is current.

workflow_phases.title: EU declaration of conformity and CE marking
objectives: Issue the EU declaration of conformity when criteria are met.
Apply CE marking as required by the legal framework.
Register the system in the EU database where applicable.

workflow_phases.title: Substantial modification change control
objectives: Detect substantial modifications that require reassessment.
Re-run scoping, dossier preparation, and internal control review where needed.

governance_boundaries: GovAI does not issue EU declarations of conformity.
GovAI does not interact with notified bodies on behalf of providers.
Repository validators check structure and presence, not legal sufficiency.
